LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT LISA NASH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE ARE LEGAL AND FACTUAL ISSUES. FACTUAL ISSUES BEING WHETHER NOTICE WAS EFFECTIVELY GIVEN SO AS TO TRIGGER HARTFORD'S OBLIGATION TO FILE A LIEN. LEGAL ISSUES ARE THAT IT IS FAR FROM CLEAR THAT HARTFORD'S CLAIM WOULD BE BARRED AND LEFT TO THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY OF THE WORKER'S COMPENSATION SYSTEM. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY; COURT REPORTER: WENDY PUN, CSR #12891

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nina Shapirshteyn of O'Reilly & Danko (650.358.5901) for defendant and moving party
Thomas Tagliarini (510.444.0692) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LISA NASH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There are legal and factual issues. Factual issues being whether notice was effectively given so as to trigger Hartford's obligation to file a lien. Legal issues are that it is far from clear that Hartford's claim would be barred and left to the exclusive remedy of the worker's compensation system. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney; Court Reporter: Wendy Pun, CSR #12891

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED, DEFENDANT WAS NOT ABLE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF BY ITS INABILITY TO CONCLUSIVELY DEMONSTRATE THAT HARTFORD DID NOT HAVE A CLAIM AGAINST THE DEFENDANT FOR RECOVERY OF SOME OR ALL OF THE PROCEEDS THAT IT MADE.
